diagrams.title: Basic Resolution Principle
diagrams.description: Shows how resolution works by taking two clauses with complementary literals and producing a resolvent. This is the fundamental inference rule in automated theorem proving where P ∨ Q and ¬P ∨ R resolve to Q ∨ R.
elements: Complementary pair: P(x) and ¬P(y) with unification x=y
diagrams.purpose: Explains the core resolution inference rule and how complementary literals are eliminated to derive new clauses

diagrams.title: Unification Process
diagrams.description: Visualizes the unification algorithm finding the Most General Unifier (MGU) that makes two terms identical. Shows variable substitutions being applied step-by-step.
elements: Unification steps showing bindings: x→a, y→b, z→b
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how unification works to find variable substitutions that make logical expressions identical, which is essential for resolution in first-order logic

diagrams.title: Resolution Unification Workflow
diagrams.description: Shows the complete cycle of resolution with unification: clauses are selected, unification finds bindings, complementary literals are removed, and new resolvents are generated. This represents the heart of automated reasoning systems.
elements: Proof search tree showing goal progression
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the complete resolution-unification cycle used in theorem provers and logic programming systems to derive new knowledge from existing facts and rules
